title: Real-time CFR with cfrnow
text: naive case fatality ratio, deaths / cases, biased downward real time: recent cases yet time die. Restricting cases resolved outcome, deaths / (deaths + recoveries), swaps upward bias, deaths resolve faster recoveries. cfrnow sidesteps never conditioning resolution. case fatal probability cfr; fatal case dies interval-censored onset--death delay F; every case still unresolved cut-right-censored, contributing mixture-cure survival term 1 - cfr * F(t). F fixed Ghani/Nishiura estimator; F co-estimated uncertainty propagated. cfrnow registered epidist model type, cfr delay take brms formulas. put covariates, smooth time effect, CFR.
code: library(cfrnow) #> Loading required package: distspec #>  #> Attaching package: 'distspec' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     Gamma, sd

title: Real-time CFR with cfrnow
text: line list needs onset_date death_date (NA cases died); optional onset_lower/onset_upper window widens onset censoring. simulate one known CFR 0.55 sampled part-way growing epidemic.

what: Preparing the data at a cut-off
title: Real-time CFR with cfrnow
text: prepare_cfr_data() classifies case observation cut-observed death, right-censored survivor, (retrospectively) fully-resolved non-death. obs_time = NULL gives retrospective fit every case resolved.
code: cutoff <- max(ll$onset_date) - 3 d <- prepare_cfr_data(ll, obs_time = cutoff) #> 35 case(s) with onset after the cut-off excluded c(cases = d$n_cases, deaths = d$n_deaths, censored = d$n_cens) #>    cases   deaths censored  #>      465      181      284 naive <- d$n_deaths / d$n_cases round(naive, 3) # the downward-biased naive ratio #> [1] 0.389

title: Real-time CFR with cfrnow
text: Supply onset--death delay cfr_prior distspec distributions. native delay parameter can Normal() prior (co-estimated) fixed number; cfr_prior Beta(), matters CFR weakly identified early . summary() reports corrected CFR delay moments (days) convergence diagnostics. corrected cfr sits naive ratio , enough resolved deaths, covers true 0.55. summary() also carries cfr_low_information attribute, TRUE CFR posterior barely moved prior, expected early outbreak, estimate prior-driven.
code: onset_to_death <- LogNormal(meanlog = Normal(2.41, 0.2), sdlog = Normal(0.51, 0.15)) fit <- fit_cfr(d, delay = onset_to_death, cfr_prior = Beta(6.6, 13.4)) summary(fit)

title: Real-time CFR with cfrnow
text: model fitted epidist, brms::bf() puts formula cfr (delay location mu). prepare_cfr_data() carries onset date onset, plus line-list columns named covariates, terms available formula. stratify group, building two groups different true CFRs recovering difference: time-varying CFR, derive time index onset column put curve . show fit recovers real signal, simulate line list whose true CFR ramps logistically 0.15 0.6 twelve onset weeks, prepare cut-three weeks past last onset, fit natural spline week. fixed-degree spline (ns(week, df = 3)) enters CFR ordinary basis columns, samples cleanly; penalised smooth (s(week)) fit , smoothing-variance parameter makes posterior harder sample. delay fixed isolate time-varying CFR: Recovering cfr(week) brms::posterior_epred(fit, dpar = "cfr") plotting posterior true curve, spline tracks ramp closely , expected, widens recent week, cases still largely censored. fit precomputed (vignette builder CmdStan); see data-raw/time_varying_cfr.R script produces cached summary read .
code: grp <- function(n, cfr, label) {   d <- simulate_linelist(n = n, cfr = cfr, delay = LogNormal(mean = 12.75, sd = 7)) |>     prepare_cfr_data(obs_time = NULL) |>     as_epidist_cure_model()   d$group <- label   d } grouped <- rbind(   grp(800, 0.2, "low"),   grp(800, 0.6, "high") ) |>   as_epidist_cure_model()  fit <- fit_cfr(grouped,   delay = onset_to_death,   formula = brms::bf(mu ~ 1, cfr ~ group) ) brms::fixef(fit) # the cfr_grouplow coefficient is negative (low group < high) true_cfr <- function(week) plogis(-1.7 + 0.19 * week) onset_date <- as.Date("2026-01-01") + sample.int(12 * 7, 4000, replace = TRUE) - 1 week <- as.numeric(onset_date - min(onset_date)) %/% 7 fatal <- runif(4000) < true_cfr(week) death_date <- as.Date(rep(NA, length(onset_date))) onset_frac <- runif(4000) death_date[fatal] <- onset_date[fatal] + floor(onset_frac[fatal] + rlnorm(sum(fatal), 2.41, 0.51)) ll_tv <- data.frame(onset_date, death_date)  cure <- prepare_cfr_data(ll_tv, obs_time = max(onset_date) + 21) |>   as_epidist_cure_model() cure$week <- as.numeric(cure$onset - min(cure$onset)) %/% 7 fit <- fit_cfr(cure,   delay = LogNormal(meanlog = 2.41, sdlog = 0.51),   formula = brms::bf(mu ~ 1, cfr ~ splines::ns(week, df = 3)) )

what: Delay family and fixing the delay
title: Real-time CFR with cfrnow
text: delay LogNormal() Gamma(); differ tail weight, sets quickly recent case counts “probably cured”. family identifiable sparse data, check sensitivity refitting . Give native parameter fixed number instead Normal() prior hold fixed; fixing whole delay gives Ghani/Nishiura estimator.
code: fit_cfr(d, delay = Gamma(shape = Normal(3.3, 1), rate = Normal(0.26, 0.08))) # gamma fit_cfr(d, delay = LogNormal(meanlog = 2.41, sdlog = 0.51)) # fixed delay

title: Real-time CFR with cfrnow
text: line list recovery_date column pass recovery_delay, fit_cfr() fits two-outcome mixture-cure model also times recoveries (reported recovery_mean/recovery_sd summary()); recovery delay may use different family death delay. sharpens cfr discharge recording reliable, actually-recovered case whose recovery unrecorded stays censored pushed toward fatal branch time, biasing cfr . without recovery_delay, recoveries treated untimed resolutions (death-default), safer discharge data patchy.
code: fit_cfr(d,   delay = onset_to_death,   recovery_delay = LogNormal(meanlog = Normal(2.9, 0.3), sdlog = Normal(0.5, 0.2)) )

title: Real-time CFR with cfrnow
text: correction fixes timing bias, data. death never reaches line list treated survivor biases cfr . Use death notification date death_date censoring absorbs reporting lag; delay model estimates onset--notification.

title: Stratified and partially-pooled CFR
text: CFR often varies group – age, treatment centre, calendar period – per-group estimate frequently required. Two limiting cases bracket options. Fitting group independently (pooling) gives high-variance estimates group’s sample small. Fitting one CFR groups (complete pooling) ignores -group variation. Partial pooling lies two: group’s CFR drawn common distribution, groups data estimated close independently groups less data shrunk toward shared mean. cfrnow epidist model, CFR takes brms formula pooling one-line change: cfr ~ (1 | group).

title: Stratified and partially-pooled CFR
text: simulate six treatment centres: two large (n = 450, 250) four small (n = 15–20), true CFRs spread around common mean. large sites pin shared mean; small ones carry little information , shrinkage acts mainly . Site labels synthetic.
code: library(cfrnow) #> Loading required package: distspec #>  #> Attaching package: 'distspec' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     Gamma, sd set.seed(20260714)  sites <- data.frame(   site = c("A", "B", "C", "D", "E", "F"),   n    = c(450, 250, 20, 15, 15, 18),   cfr  = c(0.45, 0.50, 0.30, 0.65, 0.35, 0.60) )  death_delay    <- LogNormal(mean = 11, sd = 6.5) recovery_delay <- LogNormal(mean = 16, sd = 8)  ll <- do.call(rbind, Map(function(s, n, cfr) {   cbind(     simulate_linelist(       n = n, cfr = cfr, delay = death_delay,       recovery = recovery_delay, onset_days = 45     ),     site = s   ) }, sites$site, sites$n, sites$cfr))  d <- prepare_cfr_data(ll, obs_time = as.Date("2026-02-20"), covariates = "site") c(cases = d$n_cases, deaths = d$n_deaths) #>  cases deaths  #>    768    313

what: Three fits: complete, no, and partial pooling
title: Stratified and partially-pooled CFR
text: thing changes across three fits CFR formula; delay recovery delay co-estimated . (Two short chains keep build time short; use inference.)
code: otd <- LogNormal(meanlog = Normal(2.4, 0.2), sdlog = Normal(0.5, 0.15)) otr <- LogNormal(meanlog = Normal(2.7, 0.2), sdlog = Normal(0.5, 0.15)) args <- list(   delay = otd, recovery_delay = otr, cfr_prior = Beta(1, 1),   backend = "cmdstanr", chains = 2, iter = 1000, refresh = 0, seed = 1 )  # complete pooling: a single CFR fit_pool    <- do.call(fit_cfr, c(list(d, formula = brms::bf(mu ~ 1, cfr ~ 1)), args))  # no pooling: an independent CFR per site (one estimated logit-CFR per site) fit_sep     <- do.call(fit_cfr, c(list(d, formula = brms::bf(mu ~ 1, cfr ~ 0 + site)), args))  # partial pooling: per-site CFR shrunk to a common mean fit_partial <- do.call(fit_cfr, c(list(d, formula = brms::bf(mu ~ 1, cfr ~ (1 | site))), args))

title: Stratified and partially-pooled CFR
text: complete-pool fit, summary() reports single cfr row: grouped fit reports one cfr[<site>] row per site: Collect per-site CFR rows two grouped fits figure ; complete-pool fit supplies shared mean.

title: Stratified and partially-pooled CFR
text: site shows -pool partial-pool estimate truth (×) complete-pool mean (dotted). large sites (, B) two estimates nearly coincide. small sites (C–F) -pool interval wide estimate noisy; partial pooling pulls toward mean narrows interval sharply – lower variance cost bias.
code: library(ggplot2) est$approach <- factor(est$approach, levels = c("no pool", "partial pool")) est$site <- factor(est$site, levels = rev(sites$site))  ggplot(est, aes(median, site, colour = approach)) +   geom_vline(xintercept = pool_mean, linetype = 3) +   geom_point(aes(x = truth), shape = 4, size = 2.4, colour = "grey30") +   geom_pointrange(aes(xmin = lower, xmax = upper), position = position_dodge(width = 0.55)) +   scale_y_discrete(labels = function(s) paste0(s, "  (n=", sites$n[match(s, sites$site)], ")")) +   labs(     x = "CFR: median & 95% CrI   (× = truth, dotted = complete-pool mean)",     y = NULL, colour = NULL   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Stratified and partially-pooled CFR
text: pooled onset--death delay appears irregular often mixture several populations. sites resolve different timescales, pool delay site well, retains onset--death distribution rather contributing single multimodal one:
code: fit_both <- do.call(fit_cfr, c(   list(d, formula = brms::bf(mu ~ (1 | site), cfr ~ (1 | site))), args ))

title: Stratified and partially-pooled CFR
text: Check parametric delay reproduces observed delays; real-time correction depends extrapolating delay’s tail.
code: pp_check_cfr(fit_partial, type = "delay")

what: Real-Time Case Fatality Ratio via a Mixture-Cure Survival Model
title: Real-Time Case Fatality Ratio via a Mixture-Cure Survival Model
text: Real-time case fatality ratio (CFR) estimation line-list data, using Bayesian mixture-cure survival model. cfrnow registered epidist model type, CFR onset--death delay take brms formulas. can put covariates (time-varying effect) either.

title: Real-Time Case Fatality Ratio via a Mixture-Cure Survival Model
text: Install development version GitHub pak: pak pulls packages cfrnow builds (epidist, brms, primarycensored distspec), including GitHub versions needs. Fitting runs CmdStan, also need cmdstanr CmdStan install:
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("sbfnk/cfrnow") # install.packages("cmdstanr", repos = c("https://stan-dev.r-universe.dev", getOption("repos"))) cmdstanr::check_cmdstan_toolchain() cmdstanr::install_cmdstan()

title: Real-Time Case Fatality Ratio via a Mixture-Cure Survival Model
text: naive deaths / cases ratio runs low real time, recent cases time die yet. Switching resolved cases, deaths / (deaths + recoveries), overshoots way: deaths resolve faster recoveries, resolved set top-heavy deaths. cfrnow conditions neither. case fatal probability cfr; fatal case dies interval-censored onset--death delay F; case still unresolved cut-right-censored, contributing 1 - cfr * F(t) (either non-fatal fatal yet resolved). Hold F fixed recover Ghani/Nishiura estimator (Ghani et al. 2005; Nishiura et al. 2009); model co-estimates F carries uncertainty . Give recovery dates two-outcome fit uses onset--recovery timing well. primarycensored handles onset interval-censoring real-time right-truncation F.

title: Real-Time Case Fatality Ratio via a Mixture-Cure Survival Model
text: Supply onset--death delay cfr_prior distspec distributions. native delay parameter can Normal() prior (co-estimated) fixed number (held fixed; fixing whole delay gives Ghani/Nishiura estimator). cfr_prior Beta() matters CFR weakly identified early : Beta(1, 1) uniform, Beta(1, 9) favours low CFR, Beta(6.6, 13.4) (mean 0.33) suits high-fatality pathogen. Swap family, fix delay, add recovery timing. model fitted epidist, can put covariates (smooth time) CFR via formula: Pass obs_time = NULL prepare_cfr_data() retrospective fit (reduces naive ratio delay nuisance). line list needs onset_date death_date (NA cases died). Optional onset_lower/onset_upper widen onset censoring, recovery_date column switches two-outcome fit also times recoveries. summary() reports rhat/ess_bulk (recovery_mean/_sd recoveries used) flags cfr_low_information CFR posterior barely moved prior, expected early , estimate prior-driven.
code: library(cfrnow) #> Loading required package: distspec #>  #> Attaching package: 'distspec' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     Gamma, sd  # your own onset_date / death_date line list, or a simulated one set.seed(1) ll <- simulate_linelist(   n = 400, cfr = 0.55, onset_days = 45,   delay = Gamma(mean = 12.75, sd = 7) ) d <- prepare_cfr_data(ll, obs_time = as.Date("2026-02-20")) c(cases = d$n_cases, deaths = d$n_deaths, censored = d$n_cens) #>    cases   deaths censored  #>      400      180      220 onset_to_death <- LogNormal(meanlog = Normal(2.41, 0.2), sdlog = Normal(0.51, 0.15)) fit <- fit_cfr(d, delay = onset_to_death, cfr_prior = Beta(1, 1)) summary(fit) #>     quantity   mean   q2.5    q50  q97.5  rhat ess_bulk #> 1        cfr  0.560  0.505  0.560  0.615 1.001     3100 #> 2 delay_mean 12.700 11.600 12.700 13.900 1.000     3300 #> 3   delay_sd  7.100  6.200  7.000  8.200 1.000     3000 fit_cfr(d, delay = Gamma(shape = Normal(3, 1), rate = Normal(0.25, 0.1))) # gamma fit_cfr(d, delay = LogNormal(meanlog = 2.41, sdlog = 0.51)) # fixed-F fit_cfr(d,   delay = onset_to_death,   recovery_delay = LogNormal(Normal(2.9, 0.3), Normal(0.5, 0.2)) ) # two-outcome  # covariate / time-varying CFR: carry the column through the prep, then # reference it in the formula (the onset date rides along as `onset`). dc <- prepare_cfr_data(ll, obs_time = as.Date("2026-02-20"), covariates = "age_group") fit_cfr(dc, delay = onset_to_death, formula = brms::bf(mu ~ 1, cfr ~ age_group))

title: Real-Time Case Fatality Ratio via a Mixture-Cure Survival Model
text: correction fixes timing bias; repair data. Worth reading quote number: Deaths need ascertained completely. death never reaches line list (community death outside treatment centre, say) looks like survivor drags CFR . main worry wherever ascertainment centred treatment centres. Put death notification date death_date, true date death. case counts death death_date cut-, notification date lets censoring absorb reporting lag keeps cfr correct. delay model works onset--notification, read delay_mean/delay_sd (biological delay prior) light. reporting lag, plain death dates fine. delay CFR taken stationary homogeneous unless say otherwise: one delay one CFR whole outbreak. Put formula cfr (smooth time, say) let vary. delay family modelling choice, something fit learns. Gamma lognormal differ tail weight (fast recent case counts “probably cured”), sparse data can’t tell apart, refit one check sensitivity. Recovery timing leans complete discharge data. two-outcome fit case really recovered whose recovery went unrecorded stays censored drifts toward fatal branch time, biasing cfr . death-default (recovery_date) sidesteps , prefer discharge recording patchy.

what: Build an epidist_cure_model from a line list or prepare_cfr_data() output — as_epidist_cure_model
title: Build an epidist_cure_model from a line list or prepare_cfr_data() output — as_epidist_cure_model
text: Produces one-row-per-case data frame model needs: y (delay deaths recoveries, follow-time survivors), outcome code, primary (pwindow) secondary (swindow) censoring widths. input carries timed recoveries result flagged two-outcome fit via use_recovery attribute.

title: Build an epidist_cure_model from a line list or prepare_cfr_data() output — as_epidist_cure_model
code: as_epidist_cure_model(data)

title: Build an epidist_cure_model from a line list or prepare_cfr_data() output — as_epidist_cure_model
text: data Either list returned prepare_cfr_data() data frame already carrying y, outcome, pwindow swindow.

title: Build an epidist_cure_model from a line list or prepare_cfr_data() output — as_epidist_cure_model
text: data frame class epidist_cure_model.

what: Real-time CFR as an epidist mixture-cure model — cfrnow-cure-model
title: Real-time CFR as an epidist mixture-cure model — cfrnow-cure-model
text: cfrnow registers mixture-cure survival model epidist::epidist() model type. case fatal probability cfr , fatal, dies onset--death delay; cases still alive observation cut-right-censored, corrects downward bias naive deaths / cases ratio real time. model epidist subclass, CFR delay take brms formulas, e.g. epidist(data, bf(mu ~ 1, cfr ~ age), family = lognormal()).

title: Real-time CFR as an epidist mixture-cure model — cfrnow-cure-model
text: recovery_delay, fit becomes two-outcome mixture-cure model also times recoveries: non-fatal case recovers second delay, recovered case contributes (1 - cfr) f_R(r) unresolved case cfr (1 - F_D(t)) + (1 - cfr)(1 - F_R(t)). recovery delay may use different family death delay. delay distribution's location mu (epidist expects); cure probability cfr additional dpar logit link. Supported delay families lognormal() Gamma().

what: cfrnow: Real-Time Case Fatality Ratio via a Mixture-Cure Survival Model — cfrnow-package
title: cfrnow: Real-Time Case Fatality Ratio via a Mixture-Cure Survival Model — cfrnow-package
text: Estimates real-time case fatality ratio (CFR) line-list data using Bayesian mixture-cure survival model, registered 'epidist' model type CFR onset--death delay take 'brms' formulas. case fatal probability equal CFR , fatal, dies interval-censored onset--death delay; cases still alive observation cut-right-censored, corrects downward bias naive deaths / cases ratio real time. delay co-estimated using 'primarycensored' censored-distribution machinery.

title: cfrnow: Real-Time Case Fatality Ratio via a Mixture-Cure Survival Model — cfrnow-package
text: Maintainer: Sebastian Funk sebastian.funk@lshtm.ac.uk

what: Fit the real-time mixture-cure CFR model — fit_cfr
title: Fit the real-time mixture-cure CFR model — fit_cfr
text: Estimates real-time case fatality ratio line-list data. case fatal probability cfr , fatal, dies onset--death delay; cases still alive cut-right-censored, correcting downward bias naive deaths / cases ratio. delay CFR prior given distspec distributions.

title: Fit the real-time mixture-cure CFR model — fit_cfr
code: fit_cfr(   data,   delay = LogNormal(meanlog = Normal(2, 1), sdlog = Normal(0.5, 0.3)),   cfr_prior = Beta(1, 1),   recovery_delay = NULL,   formula = mu ~ 1,   ... )

title: Fit the real-time mixture-cure CFR model — fit_cfr
text: data Output prepare_cfr_data(), epidist_cure_model / data frame y, outcome, pwindow, swindow. delay Onset--death delay distspec distribution (distspec::LogNormal() distspec::Gamma()) whose native parameters fixed numbers Normal() priors. cfr_prior CFR prior distspec::Beta(). Defaults Beta(1, 1). recovery_delay Optional onset--recovery delay (form delay) two-outcome fit; may use different family delay. formula brms formula delay location mu , optionally, CFR (cfr ~ ...). Defaults mu ~ 1. cfr_prior normally lands CFR intercept; CFR formula drops intercept (e.g. cfr ~ 0 + group, one logit-CFR per group) placed coefficients instead. applies every cfr coefficient, intercept-free formula carry factor terms. ... Passed epidist::epidist() brms::brm() (e.g. chains, iter, backend, seed).

title: Fit the real-time mixture-cure CFR model — fit_cfr
text: brmsfit class cfrnow_fit; summarise cmdstanr::summary().

title: Fit the real-time mixture-cure CFR model — fit_cfr
text: delay's native parameters may fixed number (held fixed; fixing whole delay gives Ghani/Nishiura estimator) Normal() prior (co-estimated). family (LogNormal() Gamma()) sets delay distribution. cfr_prior Beta(); matters CFR weakly identified early (Beta(1, 1) uniform, Beta(1, 9) favours low CFR, Beta(6.6, 13.4) suits high-fatality pathogen). model fitted epidist::epidist(), covariates (smooth time effect) can put CFR delay formula, e.g. formula = brms::bf(mu ~ 1, cfr ~ age). line list carries recovery dates, pass recovery_delay fit two-outcome model also times recoveries.

title: Fit the real-time mixture-cure CFR model — fit_cfr
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ll <- simulate_linelist(n = 500, cfr = 0.4, delay = LogNormal(2.4, 0.5)) d <- prepare_cfr_data(ll, obs_time = max(ll$onset_date) - 5) otd <- LogNormal(meanlog = Normal(2.41, 0.2), sdlog = Normal(0.51, 0.15))  # co-estimated delay fit <- fit_cfr(d, delay = otd, cfr_prior = Beta(1, 1), backend = "cmdstanr") summary(fit)  # fixed delay (Ghani/Nishiura), a gamma family, and a CFR covariate fit_cfr(d, delay = LogNormal(meanlog = 2.41, sdlog = 0.51)) fit_cfr(d, delay = Gamma(shape = Normal(3.3, 1), rate = Normal(0.26, 0.08))) fit_cfr(d, delay = otd, formula = brms::bf(mu ~ 1, cfr ~ group)) } # }

what: Posterior-predictive check for a cfrnow fit — pp_check_cfr
title: Posterior-predictive check for a cfrnow fit — pp_check_cfr
text: Draws replicate line-list outcomes posterior compares data model fit , replaying real-time observation process (onset--event timing cut-censoring). Two checks available: count observed deaths cut-(plus recoveries two-outcome fit), distribution observed onset--death delays.

title: Posterior-predictive check for a cfrnow fit — pp_check_cfr
code: pp_check_cfr(object, type = c("counts", "delay"), ndraws = 100)

title: Posterior-predictive check for a cfrnow fit — pp_check_cfr
text: object cfrnow_fit fit_cfr(). type check plot: "counts" (default) "delay". ndraws Number posterior draws replicate . Defaults 100, fewer fit fewer draws.

title: Posterior-predictive check for a cfrnow fit — pp_check_cfr
text: ggplot object.

title: Posterior-predictive check for a cfrnow fit — pp_check_cfr
text: check reuses fit's posterior draws CFR delay, works covariate time-varying cfr ~ ... fits well intercept-ones. needs observation cut-, fit_cfr() records data come prepare_cfr_data(); retrospective fit (obs_time = NULL) truncation replay, every fatal case shows death. quantities model generates checked: observed death (recovery) counts observed onset--death delays. split remaining cases censored versus untimed-resolved part generative model, left . check stochastic: subsamples posterior draws simulates outcomes, call set.seed() beforehand reproducible plots.

title: Posterior-predictive check for a cfrnow fit — pp_check_cfr
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ll <- simulate_linelist(n = 500, cfr = 0.4, delay = LogNormal(2.4, 0.5)) d <- prepare_cfr_data(ll, obs_time = max(ll$onset_date) - 5) fit <- fit_cfr(d, delay = LogNormal(Normal(2.4, 0.2), Normal(0.5, 0.15))) pp_check_cfr(fit, type = "counts") pp_check_cfr(fit, type = "delay") } # }

what: Build model inputs from a line list — prepare_cfr_data
title: Build model inputs from a line list — prepare_cfr_data
text: Classifies case observation cut-observed death (interval-censored onset--death delay), resolved non-death, right-censored survivor still unresolved cut-, returns pieces fit_cfr() passes Stan. case counts resolved non-death recovery_date cut-(, retrospective fit, simply never died); case contributes cure term rather censored, recording recoveries tightens estimate.

title: Build model inputs from a line list — prepare_cfr_data
code: prepare_cfr_data(   linelist,   obs_time = NULL,   covariates = character(),   t0 = NULL,   max_delay = 60 )

title: Build model inputs from a line list — prepare_cfr_data
text: linelist data frame onset_date column, optional onset_lower/onset_upper onset window, death_date column (NA cases died; use date death notified, .e. entered data, real-time censoring absorbs reporting lag), optional recovery_date column (NA unless case recorded non-fatal recovery). Dates may Date coercible. obs_time Real-time cut-(Date coercible), NULL retrospective fit every recorded death counts survivors treated fully resolved. real time, case recovery obs_time resolved; one still alive unresolved right-censored; death dated obs_time treated -yet-known (right-censored). covariates Character vector linelist column names carry per-case model rows, can used cfr ~ ... formula. onset date always carried onset; time-varying CFR derive time term (e.g. week) pass cfr ~ s(week). t0 Optional time origin (Date). Defaults min(onset) - max_delay. max_delay Plausibility filter data-entry errors, days: death record implying negative onset--death delay, one longer max_delay, dropped likely mis-keyed date. screens records; bound truncate onset--death delay model fits, set comfortably longest credible delay avoid discarding genuine long-delay deaths (bias delay short). also sets default origin, t0 = min(onset) - max_delay.

title: Build model inputs from a line list — prepare_cfr_data
text: cfrnow_data list aggregated model inputs (n_death, death_delay, death_width, n_recovery, recovery_delay, recovery_width, n_cens, censor_time, censor_width, n_resolved, n_cases, n_deaths, n_recoveries, t0, obs_time) cases data frame one row per kept case (y, outcome, pwindow, swindow, onset requested covariates), as_epidist_cure_model() turns model frame.

title: Build model inputs from a line list — prepare_cfr_data
text: Onset taken day-window [onset_lower, onset_upper] columns present (defaulting one-day window onset_date). Deaths recoveries recorded day. Records used dropped warning: missing onset, inverted onset window (onset_upper < onset_lower), death impossible onset--death delay (negative, longer max_delay), recovery dated onset. real time, cases whose onset falls obs_time yet known excluded message.

title: Build model inputs from a line list — prepare_cfr_data

what: Simulate a line list for testing and examples — simulate_linelist
title: Simulate a line list for testing and examples — simulate_linelist
text: Draws onset dates window, marks case fatal probability cfr, gives fatal cases onset--death delay drawn delay. Pass recovery delay also simulate onset--recovery times non-fatal cases add recovery_date column. Delays distspec distributions fixed parameters, matching fit_cfr() takes; can specify mean sd (e.g. LogNormal(mean = 12.75, sd = 7)). Returns line list onset_date, death_date (NA non-fatal cases) , recovery given, recovery_date (NA fatal cases). full, untruncated outcomes simulated; pass result prepare_cfr_data() obs_time induce real-time truncation.

title: Simulate a line list for testing and examples — simulate_linelist
code: simulate_linelist(   n = 200,   cfr = 0.5,   delay,   recovery = NULL,   onset_start = as.Date("2026-01-01"),   onset_days = 60 )

title: Simulate a line list for testing and examples — simulate_linelist
text: n Number cases. cfr True case fatality ratio. delay Onset--death delay: distspec distribution (distspec::LogNormal() distspec::Gamma()) fixed parameters. recovery Optional onset--recovery delay (form delay); given, non-fatal cases get recovery_date. onset_start First possible onset date. onset_days Width onset window (days); onsets uniform .

title: Simulate a line list for testing and examples — simulate_linelist
text: data frame onset_date, death_date , recovery given, recovery_date.

title: Simulate a line list for testing and examples — simulate_linelist
text: Data generated match model's daily interval-censoring: case's true onset falls uniformly within recorded day, event day floor continuous onset-plus-delay time. recorded day-level delays exactly doubly-interval-censored draw, makes simulator suitable checking calibration, rough recovery.

title: Simulate a line list for testing and examples — simulate_linelist

title: Summarise a mixture-cure CFR fit — summary.cfrnow_fit
text: Reports corrected CFR onset--death delay (mean sd, days) posterior quantiles convergence diagnostics (rhat, ess_bulk). naive deaths / cases ratio returned attribute; real time underestimates corrected CFR every fatal case died cut-.

Defaults 0.9. ascertainment_ratio Ratio r ascertainment probability fatal non-fatal cases (see Details). single positive number; defaults 1 (correction). ... Unused. x cfrnow_fit.

text: data frame one row per quantity: cfr (one cfr[<group>] row per group cfr ~ group fit), delay_mean delay_sd, carrying naive_cfr, n_cases, n_deaths, cfr_prior_sd, cfr_low_information ascertainment_ratio attributes.

title: Summarise a mixture-cure CFR fit — summary.cfrnow_fit
text: deaths resolved (young outbreak) CFR weakly identified posterior stays close prior. reported via cfr_low_information attribute: TRUE CFR posterior sd exceeds info_tol times prior sd. cfr ~ group fit CFR varies group, one cfr[<group>] row reported per group (grouping predictors must factors characters), cfr_low_information flag NA (defined single CFR). CFR model fits fatality risk among ascertained cases. ascertainment outcome-dependent – fatal non-fatal cases entering line list different rates – differs population CFR. ascertainment_ratio (r) ratio ascertainment probability fatal non-fatal cases; reported CFR shifted logit scale -log(r), r > 1 (fatal cases -ascertained) lowers r < 1 (e.g. deaths linked back cases) raises . supplied, fitted, defaults 1 (correction); correction post-hoc logit shift, sweep range r show leverage rather trusting single value.

title: cfrnow (development version)
text: pp_check_cfr() runs posterior-predictive check fit: draws replicate line-list outcomes posterior, replays real-time truncation, compares observed death counts (plus recoveries two-outcome fit) observed onset--death delays replicates (#14). summary() gains ascertainment_ratio argument corrects CFR outcome-dependent case ascertainment (fatal non-fatal cases entering line list different rates). ratio supplied, defaulting 1. fit_cfr() accepts intercept-free CFR formulas (e.g. cfr ~ 0 + group, one estimated logit-CFR per group): cfr_prior placed coefficients rather non-existent intercept, fit longer fails brms prior validation. summary() reports CFR per group cfr ~ group fit (one cfr[<group>] row per group), rather erroring silently reporting reference level.

title: cfrnow 0.1.0
text: First release. fit_cfr() estimates real-time case fatality ratio line-list data Bayesian mixture-cure survival model. registered epidist model type, CFR onset--death delay take brms formulas. prepare_cfr_data() turns line list model inputs. sorts case, chosen observation cut-, observed death, resolved non-death, right-censored survivor. onset--death delay (LogNormal Gamma) can co-estimated held fixed. Hold fixed get Ghani/Nishiura estimator. Pass recovery_date column two-outcome fit also times recoveries. Put brms formula CFR delay covariates time-varying CFR. simulate_linelist() builds line lists testing examples. summary() print() report corrected CFR, delay moments, convergence diagnostics, flag CFR weakly identified.
